ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution, Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
TENTATIVE RULING: Appearance required.

Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1.
The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom:
WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED.
Meeting ID: 160 225 4765
Password: 514879
To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.
EXAMINER: SEAN BANG

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Inventory And Appraisal
Off Calendar May-04-2026 Text Ruling

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution, Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar May-04-2026 Continued To Jun-08-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance via Zoom:
Edward Higginbotham, Esq. for Michael Kellough, Administrator.

The Court inquired about counsel's failure to appear. Mr. Higginbotham stated that he was in Department 514 in Oakland, that he could not appear on March 30, 2026, and that he did not know that he could email this court for a continuance. The Court stated that it is not going to impose sanctions and noted that counsel knows he is required to inform the court if he cannot appear. Mr. Higginbotham addressed the checks listed in the Inventory and Appraisal stating that they came from the decedent's Wells Fargo bank accounts and that the checks are the whole of the estate. Mr. Higginbotham noted that he is not a probate attorney. The Court stated that petition to terminate the estate needs to be filed.

A status hearing on the filing of a petition to terminate the estate/final distribution is set for June 8, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Guitron. Matter not reported.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-04-2026 Text Ruling

Off Calendar Mar-30-2026 Continued To May-04-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The matter was called for hearing on 1) Status on Filing Inventory and Appraisal; and 2) Status on Close of Estate. No appearances were made by any party or counsel.

The Court continued the matter to May 4, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and stated that an Order to Show Cause re: Sanctions shall issue to attorney Edward Higginbotham for failure to appear.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution, Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Nov-17-2025 Continued To Jan-26-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Edward Higginbotham for administrator Michael Kellough (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on 1) Status on Filing Inventory and Appraisal; and 2) Status on Close of Estate.

Mr. Higginbotham reported that he had recently filed a written update and is in the process of completing the Inventory and Appraisal. He stated that once that is completed, he intends to file a petition to close the estate for lack of assets.

The Court continued the matter to January 26, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution, Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Aug-11-2025 Continued To Nov-17-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Edward Higginbotham appeared via zoom for Michael Kellough (petitioner).

Mr. Higginbotham stated that he had an emergency medication, that he was short staff, and that he didn't have time to alert the Court of his absence. The order to show cause is ordered off calendar.

The Court suggested the filing of a small estate affidavit, that the assets may be under $120,000.00. Mr. Higginbotham stated that it may even be under $50,000.00 in assets. The matters are continued to November 17, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution, Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Jun-16-2025 Continued To Aug-11-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearances.

The Court continued the matter to August 11, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, due to nonappearance.

The Court shall issue an Order to Appear/Order to Show Cause to attorney Edward Higginbotham to explain why sanctions in the amount of $250 should not be imposed, pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Sec. 128, for failure to appear.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Edward Higginbotham for Michael Kellough, Administrator (via Zoom).

Mr. Higginbotham reported progress in obtaining financial records but encountered delays with Wells Fargo. An appointment was scheduled but postponed due to Mr. Kellogg's medical complications. Mr. Kellough is now recovering and expects to be able to reschedule the appointment within a few weeks.

The court continued the matter to May 7, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Issuance Of Letters
Off Calendar Jul-08-2024 Continued To Aug-19-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-22-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Edward Higginbotham appeared by zoom for Michael Kellough.

Discussion was held regarding the defects in the petition, there being $50,000.00 or less in the bank account, Mr. Higginbotham wanting to give Mr. Kellough access to the funds, but not knowing how, what the decedent would have wanted, Mr. Higginbotham having contacted a probate attorney. etc. The court explained the probate process, how pay on death accounts are handled, the purpose and process of an inventory and appraisal, the probate referee's role, etc. The court waives all defects. The Petition for Letters of Administration is under submission with the court's intent to grant. Status on the issuance of letters is set for July 8, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of the inventory and appraisal is set for September 23, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A status hearing for filing of a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for June 16, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Bond is waived. The assigned probate referee is to be determined.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-25-2023 Continued To Dec-20-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance via Zoom:
Edward M. Higginbotham, Esq. for Michael Kellough, Petitioner;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Mr. Higginbotham stated that he had reviewed the tentative ruling and asked if he would be required to publish each time the matter is continued. The Court stated that if an amended petition is filed then it would be required. The Court noted that it thought that a supplement would be filed not an amended petition. The Court and counsel briefly confer regarding a small estate affidavit. Mr. Higginbotham stated that he only has an estimate from Mr. Kellough, addressed item six in the notes stating that he will contact the probate examiner for clarification, that he has explained to his client that he doesn't do this type of work, and that his client insists that he handle this matter.

The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to December 20, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: R. Cabrera. Matter not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-17-2022 Continued To Dec-21-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Edward Higginbotham, via Zoom, for Michael Kellough, Petitioner.

Counsel advises of working on clearing the Examiner's notes specifically regarding proof of publication and that he hopes to be ready to proceed within 30-45 days. The court notes the interesting nature of this matter and queries counsel regarding whether decedent's sister, Lisa Black, is aware of this proceeding. Counsel advises that Lisa Black is aware of this matter and of her brother's relationship with the decedent and that he has a declaration from her stating that she is considering waiving her rights to any inheritance. The court advises counsel that it wants confirmation that all parties have been advised of their rights regarding this estate and instructs counsel to pay close attention to the formalities of CA Law especially regarding statements submitted to the court under the penalty of perjury.

The matter is continued to Dec-12-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cristobal. Not Reported.
